Records.authors: de la Cruz, Leonardo, Vanhove, Pierre
Records.description: Three-loop equal-mass banana in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. Third-order epsilon-deformed ODE. K3 surface (CY 2-fold). Algorithm applied up to 20-loop order.

Three-loop equal-mass banana graph in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. The underlying geometry is a K3 surface whose Picard-Fuchs operator is the symmetric square of the equal-mass sunrise elliptic operator. 4 master integrals. Two new iterated integral functions $G_1$ and $G_2$ are needed for $\varepsilon$-factorisation ($G_1$ is an integral of $\varpi_0^2$ times a rational function; $G_2$ is an iterated integral involving $G_1$). Griffiths transversality conditions used to eliminate second derivatives of the period. Reproduces the result of Pogel-Wang-Weinzierl.

Three-loop equal-mass banana integral in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. Related to a Calabi-Yau 2-fold (K3 surface). The Picard-Fuchs operator in $D=2$ is a symmetric square, so it can be treated with methods similar to the elliptic case. Singular points at $x \in \{4,16\}$. Four master integrals. The $\varepsilon$-factorised differential equation was previously given in arXiv:2207.12893. First appearance of non-polylogarithmic differential one-forms (quasi-Eichler) at modular weight 2.

Three-loop banana in $D=2$ with all four internal masses equal. The period vector reduces to three dimensions ($\Pi_2, \Pi_{11}, \Pi_0$). The transcendental lattice is $T = H \oplus \langle 12\rangle$. The maximal cut coincides with products of maximal cuts of the (two-loop) sunrise integral, and the periods and mirror map admit an ordinary modular parametrisation (elliptic, upper half-plane $H$).
Records.familyName: three-loop banana (equal masses) / three-loop equal-mass banana (elliptic modular periods)
Records.method: K3 periods, mirror map, modular parametrisation, reduction to sunrise maximal cut
Records.location: section 'Automorphic properties of three-loop banana integrals', subsection 'The equal-mass case m1=m2=m3=m4' (bananas_2.tex)

Records.description: Three-loop equal-mass banana in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. New integral representation via Feynman-parameter pairing trick splitting 4 propagators into 2 pairs. Eps-form DE with 5-letter alphabet; iterated integrals of elliptic type, arbitrary order in $\varepsilon$.

Three-loop massive banana (sunrise) self-energy with four unit-mass propagators, external momentum $2p$ with $p$ on shell ($p^2=1$), at pseudo-threshold $(2p)^2=4m^2$. Three masters solved via DRA using meromorphic homogeneous solutions of a second-order dimensional recurrence; $\varepsilon$ expansion near $d=3$ in $\operatorname{Li}_k(1/3), \operatorname{Li}_k(2/3), \zeta(3)$.
Equal-mass 3-loop sunrise (banana) with one off-shell external leg. Four equal-mass propagators.
Three-loop equal-mass banana sector with $\nu=(1,0,0,0,1,0,1,1,0)$. $E_1(2;t)$ satisfies 3rd-order Picard-Fuchs ODE. Expressed via elliptic trilogarithms at 6th roots of unity.
Three-loop equal-mass banana in $D=2$. Calabi-Yau geometry. Instanton expansion with Frobenius correction.
Records.familyName: 3-loop equal-mass sunset in D=2 / 3-loop equal-mass sunset in D=4-2eps

Records.authors: Broedel, Johannes, Duhr, Claude, Matthes, Nils
Records.description: Three-loop equal-mass banana self-energy integral. The Picard-Fuchs operator is a third-order Fuchsian operator that is the symmetric square of a second-order operator. Master integrals satisfy a $3\times 3$ system of first-order differential equations. After modular parametrisation with $\Gamma_1(6)$ and change of basis using meromorphic modular forms from Theorem 3.1, the differential equation involves meromorphic modular forms for $\Gamma_1(6)$ with poles at $\xi = \pm 3$. The equation is NOT in canonical form due to $\varepsilon$-dependent entries and higher-order poles. Complete analytic results through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^2)$ obtained; results available from authors.

3-loop equal-mass sunrise (banana) integral in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. 4 massive propagators. Symbol and coaction constructed via unipotent differential equations with Frobenius basis. Calabi-Yau 2-fold geometry.

Records.authors: Bloch, Spencer, Kerr, Matt, Vanhove, Pierre
Records.description: Three-loop banana self-energy in $d=2$ with four equal-mass propagators. Graph hypersurface is a K3 surface (Picard number 19). Result expressed via elliptic trilogarithms. At $t=0$: $7\zeta(3)$. Confirms Broadhurst conjecture relating $t=1$ value to L-function of K3.
Three-loop equal-mass banana graph. $3\times 3$ coupled DE equivalent to third-order ODE (symmetric square). Homogeneous solutions are products of complete elliptic integrals.

Equal-mass three-loop banana (sunset) graph family with 4 massive propagators of equal mass $m$ in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. Parametrized by $t = p_1^2/m^2$. 4 master integrals with 3 coupled in the top sector. Associated with a Calabi-Yau 2-fold. Boundary conditions via expansion by regions at $t \to -\infty$.

Records.authors: Bönisch, Kilian, Duhr, Claude, Fischbach, Fabian, Klemm, Albrecht, Nega, Christoph
Records.description: Three-loop equal-mass banana in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. Associated CY geometry is K3 surface with Picard number 19. 3rd-order PF operator is symmetric square of 2-loop operator. Weight-3 iterated Eisenstein series.

Records.authors: Broedel, Johannes, Duhr, Claude, Dulat, Falko, Marzucca, Robin, Penante, Brenda, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Three-loop banana (watermelon) graph with four equal non-zero internal masses. 4 master integrals (3 non-trivial + 1 tadpole). Full analytic result at $\varepsilon=0$ in $d=2-2\varepsilon$ as iterated integrals of modular forms for $\Gamma_1(6)$ and as elliptic multiple polylogarithms. Uniform transcendental weight 1.
Records.location: eq.(2.1), Section 2; results eq.(4.4), eq.(4.8), eq.(5.14)

Records.authors: Dlapa, Christoph, Henn, Johannes M., Wagner, Fabian J.
Records.description: Three-loop equal-mass banana graph (4 massive propagators connecting 2 vertices) in $D=2-2\varepsilon$. Picard-Fuchs equation is third-order; solutions are products of sunrise second-order solutions.

Records.authors: Pögel, Sebastian, Wang, Xing, Weinzierl, Stefan
Records.description: Three-loop equal-mass banana in $D=2-2\varepsilon$. 4 massive propagators, K3 geometry. Epsilon-factorised DE with 6-letter alphabet of meromorphic modular forms for $\Gamma_1(6)$. Results to $\varepsilon^6$.

Three-loop equal-mass banana (sunrise-type) two-point self-energy graph with four massive propagators of common mass $m$, external momentum squared $p^2 = s$. Symanzik polynomials $U = e_3(\alpha)$ and $F = \alpha_1 \alpha_2 \alpha_3 \alpha_4 s - U m^2 \sum \alpha_i$. The Euler homogeneity annihilator and one degree-4 annihilator generate a D-module of holonomic rank $r=4$, with singular locus $s m^2 (s-4 m^2)(s-16 m^2)$. A fourth-order Picard-Fuchs operator factorizes as $(\partial_s + r_1)(\partial_s^3 + r_2 \partial_s^2 + r_3 \partial_s + r_4)$. The family is known to involve Calabi-Yau/elliptic-type periods (K3 geometry).
Records.location: Section Applications c), around Eqs. for 3_loop_banana, eq:ann_3_loop_banana, eq:pf_3l_banana

Records.description: Three-loop banana (watermelon) self-energy with four equal-mass propagators connecting two vertices, treated in $d=4+\varepsilon$ with two non-canonical power configurations. After partial quadratization the integral is reduced to Lauricella $F_D^{(2)}$/Fox function form; the massless limit is analyzed explicitly. The equal-mass banana is known to require Calabi-Yau three-fold periods.

Equal-mass three-loop two-point banana (watermelon) with four propagators connecting the two external vertices. Analytic Laurent coefficients $C_{-2}, C_{-1}, C_0$ derived in Eq. (2.11) via a simplified sector decomposition; $C_1$ obtained numerically and cross-checked with pySecDec. Called `Loop (I)` in the paper.

Banana family, banana_L3_equal-mass-internal. Banana (sunrise) graph: two vertices joined by L+1 edges, L loops, equal internal masses. Evaluates to Calabi-Yau (L-1)-fold periods; GKZ systems give the all-L structure; eps-factorized at all L.

Records.authors: Fevola, C., Mizera, S., Telen, S.
Records.description: Landau singularities of the 4-banana with equal internal, zero external masses, computed via principal Landau determinants.

corrections.note: v1.2.3 release curation: re-keyed from non-canonical home

Names: Banana, equal-mass propagators, equal external masses
